{{Top|ga|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:SupplyShip1729-SWBH.jpg]] |name=''1729'' |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type= |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem=[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Equipped]]''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor=[[Life-form scanner/Legends|Lifeform scanner]] |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=At least 2 |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role=[[Supply ship/Legends|Supply ship]] |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles= |affiliation=[[Desolation Alley]] |navy= |fleet= |owners= |captains=[[Unidentified captain (1729)|Captain]] |crewmembers=[[1729's co-pilot|Co-pilot]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Captain, uh, my scopes just picked up a ghost image in our sensor wake.''"
"''Probably another glitch like that false biosignature we saw in the cargo hold earlier.|The co-pilot and captain of ''1729'' are duped by Jango Fett|}} '''''1729''''' was a [[Supply ship/Legends|supply craft]] for the [[Desolation Alley]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] facility on the [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. [[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty hunters]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] and [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] both made use of the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to infiltrate Desolation Alley. Wesell stowed away in ''1729''{{'s}} cargo hold while Fett concealed [[Jaster's Legacy|his ship]] in the supply ship's [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] wake on approach to Oovo IV. ''1729''{{'s}} sensors reported both events, but the crew of the ship dismissed both as sensor glitches, and the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters were able to infiltrate the prison facility. ==Description== A [[Supply ship/Legends|supply ship]], ''1729'' was larger than an [[Amphibious Interstellar Assault Transport/infantry|AIAT/i]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]]Although no dimensions are given for ''1729'', ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' depicts an [[Amphibious Interstellar Assault Transport/infantry|AIAT/i]] immediately behind the supply ship, and ''1729'' can easily be seen to be larger.—which had a wingspan of thirty-one [[Meter/Legends|meters]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''—and was crewed by at least two people. The hull was flat on the underside of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and painted a shade of [[Color/Legends|yellow]]. The [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] was located high on the bow of the ship and offset to the left to accommodate a [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]] to the right; the [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] array being augmented by a [[Rectenna/Legends|rectenna]] atop the vessel. ''1729'' was propelled by three [[Sublight engine/Legends|thrusters]] on the aft of the ship; one larger located centrally, and a smaller thruster on either side. The supply ship was equipped with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], as well as a [[Life-form scanner/Legends|lifeform scanner]] in the cargo hold. ==History== [[File:ThatsNoGhostImageItsAJango-SWBH.jpg|thumb|left|270px|''Jaster{{'s}} Legacy'' hides behind ''1729''.]] ''1729'' was used for delivering supplies to [[Desolation Alley]], a [[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|Republic Correctional Authority]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on the large [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]]. On one particular supply run in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the supply craft's sensors reported a biosignature in the cargo hold. The crew dismissed the anomaly as a glitch, not knowing that it was caused by the [[Clawdite/Legends|Clawdite]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]], who had stowed aboard. The supply ship exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] at Oovo IV for a standard approach, the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] around the prison being lowered for ''1729'' to pass though. The [[1729's co-pilot|co-pilot]] noticed a ghost image in their sensor wake, which, unbeknownst to the crew, was caused by [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], another bounty hunter. Fett used [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] to keep his ship, ''[[Jaster's Legacy]]'', directly behind ''1729''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] so that he could infiltrate Desolation Alley. Assuming the ghost image to be related to the biosignature reported earlier, the [[Unidentified captain (1729)|captain]] told their co-pilot to check again. However, Fett had already split off from ''1729''{{'s}} trajectory, and the anomaly was dismissed as another glitch, allowing Fett to land undetected. The supply ship landed at Desolation Alley's [[Spaceport/Legends|supply port]] and unloaded its cargo before taking off sometime later. ==Commanders and crew== At the helm of ''1729'' was the captain and their co-pilot. On approach to Desolation Alley, the captain communicated with the prison [[Desolation Alley landing controller|landing controller]] while the co-pilot checked the ship's sensors. ==Behind the scenes== ''1729'' first appeared in the [[2002]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', during an in-game cutscene providing an introduction to the "Asteroid Prison" chapter of the game. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{CSWECite|II||''Jaster's Legacy''}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of 1729}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Versorgungsschiff 1729 }} [[Category:Starships of the Galactic Republic]] [[Category:Transports]]
